_comment: MARCUS Echocardiogram Q&A generation templates. Used by scripts/build_dataset.py.
generation_guidance: Echocardiogram clips are multi-view 2D studies extracted from DICOM files. Standard views available: parasternal long axis (PLAX), parasternal short axis (PSAX), apical 4-chamber (A4C), apical 2-chamber (A2C), and subcostal. Questions must reference specific visual findings visible in the clip. Focus on: LV systolic function (EF, wall motion abnormalities, regional RWMA), RV size/function (RV:LV ratio, TAPSE, systolic excursion), valvular disease (mitral stenosis/regurgitation, aortic stenosis/regurgitation, tricuspid, pulmonary), pericardial effusion (size, tamponade physiology, stranding), cardiomyopathies (dilated, hypertrophic, restrictive, ARVC, Takotsubo), and structural abnormalities.

example_questions.question: Based on this apical 4-chamber view, how would you grade the left ventricular systolic function?
Which left ventricular wall segment demonstrates a regional wall motion abnormality in this clip?
What valvular abnormality is demonstrated in this parasternal long-axis view?
What is the most significant finding visible in the pericardial space?
mcq_example: Large circumferential pericardial effusion with tamponade physiology

pretrain_focus: Structured echo report descriptions covering chamber dimensions, LV/RV function, valve assessments, and overall impression — mirroring a standard echo report structure.
sft_focus: Diverse Q&A covering all echo views and pathologies. Questions should require visual interpretation of the clip.
grpo_focus: Unambiguous MCQ questions about specific, visually verifiable findings. Distractors should be clinically plausible but clearly distinguishable from the correct answer on careful review.
